unit_14 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_14

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 262 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
According to Merrin, many law school curricula are(i) the challenge of fostering an' adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with,core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a(ii) ethics, thus creating an(iii) audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.According to Merrin, many law school curricula are(i) the challenge of fostering an' adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with,core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a(ii) ethics, thus creating an(iii) audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　unequal to; cynical sense of; unfavorable
fosterethics
foster: standard unit headword | ethics: standard unit headword
#2 Text Completion
Reviews written by music critic and composer Stephenson were hardly (i)______: musicians who performed his music could count on sympathetic coverage, while those who ignored him were held to (ii)______ standards.Reviews written by music critic and composer Stephenson were hardly (i)______: musicians who performed his music could count on sympathetic coverage, while those who ignored him were held to (ii)______ standards.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　disinterested; exacting
exactexacting
exact: standard unit headword | exacting: standard unit headword
#3 Sentence Equivalence
Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had ______ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had ______ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.
答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable
plotinsignificant
plot: standard unit headword | insignificant: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　sketchy; an exhaustive
cavalierto be sure
cavalier: standard unit headword | to be sure: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　profligate; extravagant
erraticplanetary
erratic: standard unit headword | planetary: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　questionable; inherently implausible about; conjecture

However, they simultaneously have (i)______ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii)______ such fundamental skills truly are.Computers have become adept in rarefied domains once thought to be uniquely human. However, they simultaneously have (i)______ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii)______ such fundamental skills truly are.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　failed to master; impressive
rarefiedmimic
rarefied: standard unit headword | mimic: standard unit headword

Performance art is __________ term: widely divergent artistic activities harbor under it.Performance art is __________ term: widely divergent artistic activities harbor under it.
答案：B　an accommodating
harbor
harbor: standard unit headword
#9 Sentence Equivalence
The difficulty of reforming electoral politics is not lack of the right tools but the need to put them into the hands of impartial agents: the goal should be to build capacity while ______ partisanship.The difficulty of reforming electoral politics is not lack of the right tools but the need to put them into the hands of impartial agents: the goal should be to build capacity while ______ partisanship.
答案：B, D　constraining; deterring
deterfacilitate
deter: standard unit headword | facilitate: standard unit headword
#10 SE
The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.
答案：A, D　inimical to; adverse to
paradox
paradox: standard unit headword

It is hard to write a good novel that takes a strong stand on a social issue, although there are a few novelists, Zola and Norris for example, who do manage to (i)_________ the often circumscribing effects of (ii)_______.

Blank 2
答案：C, E　transcend; polemic
circumscribe
circumscribe: standard unit headword
#12 Sentence Equivalence
Liam Clancy described the young Bob Dylan as a sponge, eagerly absorbing the possibilities life and culture might provide, and Dylan presents himself so in his memoir ______ in his intellectual and musical curiosity.Liam Clancy described the young Bob Dylan as a sponge, eagerly absorbing the possibilities life and culture might provide, and Dylan presents himself so in his memoir ______ in his intellectual and musical curiosity.
答案：A, E　insatiable; voracious
voraciousdevious
voracious: standard unit headword | devious: standard unit headword

Despite the fact that some of the company's hiring and promotion practices have been widely __________, these practices still persist in certain divisions of the company.Despite the fact that some of the company's hiring and promotion practices have been widely __________, these practices still persist in certain divisions of the company.
答案：C　proscribed
persist
persist: standard unit headword
#14 TC TRIPLE
When the introduction of a nonnative species into a habitat correlates with the decline of a species native to that habitat, observers may (i) __________ a causal relationship. However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. When native species do decline after nonnative species are introduced, the reason may be a human-caused habitat alteration that affects native and nonnative species (iii) __________.When the introduction of a nonnative species into a habitat correlates with the decline of a species native to that habitat, observers may (i) __________ a causal relationship. However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. When native species do decline after nonnative species are introduced, the reason may be a human-caused habitat alteration that affects native and nonnative species (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　infer; unusual; differentially
habitat
habitat: standard unit headword

Blank 2

答案：B, E　extolled; acclaimed

#19 TC TRIPLE
Reading Frankfurt's book. I worried that in the absence of specifie examples or nuaneed analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt's book. I worried that in the absence of specifie examples or nuaneed analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror
vilify

答案：A, B　implacable; unyielding

ensue

Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　monster; prickly; sympathetic

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　license for fiction; fictive; integral

答案：B, F　collective; social

Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious
transient

Blank 2

答案：C, F　detracting; deprecatory

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity

答案：B, D　arrogant; hubristic

If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)______, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)______.Given our species’ increasing numbers and appetites which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change, even (i)______ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. 答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned

One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. Compared to extreme weather events such as tornadoes, tropical storms, and severe floods, droughts appear almost (i) : they (ii) of storms and floods, appearing instead as “slow-motion” disasters whose beginnings and ends are often hard to identify.Compared to extreme weather events such as tornadoes, tropical storms, and severe floods, droughts appear almost (i) : they (ii) of storms and floods, appearing instead as “slow-motion” disasters whose beginnings and ends are often hard to identify.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　prosaic; lack the violent destructiveness

match: standard unit headword

答案：C, F　an exception; a singularity

#105 TC TRIPLE
Unlike news reports, theater isn’t expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i)_______, the form, by nature traffics in (ii)_______. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what’s real and what’s (iii)_______.Unlike news reports, theater isn’t expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i)_______, the form, by nature traffics in (ii)_______. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what’s real and what’s (iii)_______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　pretense; illusion; invented
paradox
paradox: standard unit headword

答案：C, F　renounce; disavow

Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　despite; disarms; ignored

答案：C, F　speculation; conjecture

答案：B, D　quotidian; prosaic

#128 Text Completion
At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique

答案：B, C　expedite; facilitate

Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii)______ in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. Thus, relative to large craters, small craters are much less (iii)______ on bodies with dense atmosphere, such as Earth, Venus, and Titan, than they are on Mercury and Moon.Substantial atmosphere around planetary bodies acts as (i)______ incoming objects. Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii)______ in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. 答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude

Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook

答案：C, F　exacerbated; aggravated

Some fish farmers use upwards of five kilograms of wild fish as feed to grow one kilogram of carnivorous species like sea bass. Such (ii)______ feed plus other environmental side effects undermine the expected (iii)______ fish farming.Overall, fish farming may provide a (i)______ wild fish stocks, but for certain species, the farmers cause a net loss of wild fish. Some fish farmers use upwards of five kilograms of wild fish as feed to grow one kilogram of carnivorous species like sea bass. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.Traditional Vietnamese culture has long promoted the idea of gender equality. Founding myths (i)______ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.

答案：A　diffident

#164 Text Completion
One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. The process is made easier when what we are learning (ii)______ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. The process is made easier when what we are learning (ii)______ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　innate; harmonizes with; adapt
exact

答案：C, E　obviate; displace

答案：C, E　private; insular

Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)______ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)______ . People reasonably (iii)______ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)______ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)______ . People reasonably (iii)______ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.

Blank 2

答案：A, D, I　surfeit; virtue; forgo

答案：D, F　repudiate; recant

Carving materials such as stone, bone, antler, wood, and ivory were (iii)______, but paper and drawing tools were unknown until introduced by early explorers and missionaries.Inuit print making is less (i)______ than carving in that it does not have substantial historical precedents, although there are (ii)______ incised carvings on bone or antler, facial tattoo marks, or inlay skin work on clothing, mitts and footwear. Blank 2

答案：A, B　a rejection of; a liberation from

Contrary to those who fear the impact of invasive species on native plants, the biologists contend that the threat posed to biodiversity by nonnative species is often (i)______ . For instance, a study of garlic mustard, a nonnative plant now thriving in Minnesota’s oak forests, found that garlic mustard abundance in forest plots was not (ii)______ the number of other plant species there.Contrary to those who fear the impact of invasive species on native plants, the biologists contend that the threat posed to biodiversity by nonnative species is often (i)______ . For instance, a study of garlic mustard, a nonnative plant now thriving in Minnesota’s oak forests, found that garlic mustard abundance in forest plots was not (ii)______ the number of other plant species there.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　exaggerated; related to
plot
plot: standard unit headword

But as temperatures rise the permafrost recedes, (i)______ the (ii)______ of forest.

Blank 2
答案：B, E　facilitating; incursion

The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.

答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

When pulsars were first discovered, some of the astrophysicists involved briefly (i)______ these types of stars might be (ii)______ extraterrestrial intelligence. Specifically, the pulsar’s periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii)______ rather than—as it turned out to be—the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.When pulsars were first discovered, some of the astrophysicists involved briefly (i)______ these types of stars might be (ii)______ extraterrestrial intelligence. Specifically, the pulsar’s periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii)______ rather than—as it turned out to be—the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　thought that; suggestive of; a constructed code
disavow
disavow: standard unit headword

答案：B, D, I　thought that; suggestive of; a constructed code

#255 Text Completion
Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)______ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)______ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)______ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)______ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　a significant struggle; entails

#256 Text Completion
Hyana Kusiemko and her colleagues speculate that the (i)______ support among low-income workers for increases in the minimum wage is a form of last-place aversion: people who are in a marginally better position than the worst off seek to (ii)______ to distinguish themselves from those in last place.Hyana Kusiemko and her colleagues speculate that the (i)______ support among low-income workers for increases in the minimum wage is a form of last-place aversion: people who are in a marginally better position than the worst off seek to (ii)______ to distinguish themselves from those in last place.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　tepid; retain their ability

Business leaders who prefer centralized, pyramidal managerial structures to diversity and competition tend to (i)______ dissent in the decision-making process. But this insistence on (ii)______ can be dangerous in that it deprives decision makers of a full range of alternatives.Business leaders who prefer centralized, pyramidal managerial structures to diversity and competition tend to (i)______ dissent in the decision-making process. But this insistence on (ii)______ can be dangerous in that it deprives decision makers of a full range of alternatives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　deprecate; unanimity

Their joy in movement is so pure, so complete and so (ii)______.

答案：C, F　delightful; contagious

#259 Text Completion
Invention was (i)______ the work of the ancient Greek historians, whose writings were filled with long and often purely fictitious speeches by great historical figures. The animating force in historical writing was rhetoric rather than (ii)______. Even well into the eighteenth century, not a few historians continued to understand themselves as artists, given a license to invent.Invention was (i)______ the work of the ancient Greek historians, whose writings were filled with long and often purely fictitious speeches by great historical figures. The animating force in historical writing was rhetoric rather than (ii)______. Even well into the eighteenth century, not a few historians continued to understand themselves as artists, given a license to invent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　a hallmark of; evidence

#260 Text Completion
Greenhouse gases emitted into atmosphere are virtually permanent, and because they (i)______ in the atmosphere, their effects is (ii)______ . These facts necessitate that policies related to greenhouse gases differ from policies related to various pollutants whose effects are (iii)______ and often temporary.Greenhouse gases emitted into atmosphere are virtually permanent, and because they (i)______ in the atmosphere, their effects is (ii)______ . These facts necessitate that policies related to greenhouse gases differ from policies related to various pollutants whose effects are (iii)______ and often temporary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　disperse evenly; global; local

#261 Text Completion
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______ : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______ : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for
flee
flee: standard unit headword
#262 Text Completion
Research note that wolves’ otherwise strongly hierarchical society is marked by occasional displays of populist (i)______ : if a pack leader proves a too-snappish tyrant, subordinate wolves will (ii)______ the top cur.Research note that wolves’ otherwise strongly hierarchical society is marked by occasional displays of populist (i)______ : if a pack leader proves a too-snappish tyrant, subordinate wolves will (ii)______ the top cur.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　umbrage; collectively overthrow
